location.office_time_required: at least 2 days/week in-person at NYC or SF offices

seniority.level: unclear (all levels; minimum 4 years PM experience)
level_signals: Minimum 4 years of product management or adjacent product-building experience
Leads small cross-functional teams of engineers and designers

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: BA/BS in a technical or analytical field (e.g. Computer Science, Engineering, Information Systems, Analytics, Mathematics, Physics, Applied Sciences, or related)
Minimum 4 years of product management or adjacent product-building experience.
Strong customer empathy and design thinking
Passion for AI, shown through hands-on building, prototyping, or side projects, and staying current on research and tools.
Fluency with core LLM concepts and systems, such as prompting, fine-tuning, embeddings, retrieval, and evaluation, and the judgment to translate these into reliable, user-facing products.
Fluency with modern prototyping tools (e.g. Cursor, Bolt, Claude Code) and comfort with design/collaboration environments like Figma and Git.
Fluency in data and experimentation, and understanding when the answer cannot be found in the data
Proven ability to lead small, cross-functional teams toward ambitious goals with urgency and agency.
Ability to translate between technical and non-technical audiences, synthesizing complex systems into compelling narratives.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience building in financial, enterprise, or other domains requiring complex systems thinking.

scope.surface: Multiple product areas: Spend Management, Procurement, Treasury, Bill Payments, Risk, Growth, Accounting, Travel
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS, finance infrastructure for businesses
primary_metrics_inferred: Customer spend managed / annualized spend volume
core_responsibilities: Own the vision, strategy, and roadmap for a Ramp product
Drive high-velocity execution with a world-class team of engineers and designers, balancing principled intuition with data-driven decision making.
Marry customer insights with technical possibilities, crafting intuitive user experiences that abstract away complexity
Prototype rapidly using tools like Cursor and Bolt to accelerate the product development lifecycle and fast-forward to production-ready products.
Stay deeply plugged into AI research, industry trends, and emergent applications, filtering signal from noise and applying it creatively to Ramp's product
Contribute to the evolution of Ramp's product management practices as we scale AI across our platform.

green_flags: General posting covering multiple product areas — broad opportunity for fit
Strong product mission with concrete metrics ($100B annualized spend, 50,000+ companies)
Explicitly states 'All Levels' — accessible to a range of experience levels
Emphasis on agency and ownership ('everyone is a builder who owns problems end to end')
Relocation support to NYC or SF offered
Comprehensive benefits including medical, dental, vision, 401(k), parental leave
Unlimited AI token usage as a benefit — signals genuine AI-forward culture
red_flags: Hybrid requirement (2 days/week) limits fully remote candidates; NYC or SF offices only
General/evergreen posting — no specific team or product area named, making it hard to evaluate exact fit

ambiguities: Archetype: JD title says 'Generalist' but the body heavily emphasizes AI product fluency (LLMs, prototyping with AI tools), making AI Product Manager also a strong fit. Classified as AI Product Manager with secondary Generalist PM.
Seniority is 'All Levels' with only a minimum of 4 years stated — no maximum or level brackets defined. Could span mid-level to Staff/Principal.
